explanation: The LTL property states that globally (G), if the self-test fails (selfTestFail becomes true), then from that point onwards, ventilation (ventilating) must never occur (G !ventilating). This correctly captures the requirement that if the SelfTest fails, the controller shall not be able to proceed to ventilation at any future point in time.

The natural language statement specifies a safety condition: if the self-test fails, ventilation must not occur. The LTL property captures this by stating that globally (G), if the `selfTestFail` proposition becomes true at any point, then from that moment onwards, it must always be the case (`G`) that the system is not `ventilating` (`!ventilating`). This ensures that ventilation is permanently prevented once a self-test failure is detected. The `SelfTestMode` context is implicitly covered by the `selfTestFail` proposition, as a self-test can only fail when it is being performed in that mode.

The LTL property states that globally (G), if the system is in `SelfTestMode` and `selfTestFail` occurs, then it must globally (G) be the case that `ventilating` is false, meaning ventilation must never proceed. This precisely captures the natural language requirement: "If the SelfTest fails, the controller shall not be able to proceed to ventilation" within the context of "SelfTest Mode".

The property states that globally, if the SelfTest fails (represented by 'selfTestFail'), then ventilation (represented by 'ventilating') must never occur in the future. The 'G' operator ensures this condition holds at all times. The implication '->' connects the failure of the self-test to the subsequent absence of ventilation. The '!F' operator asserts that 'ventilating' will never eventually become true after the 'selfTestFail' event.

The LTL property states that globally, if the system is in 'SelfTestMode' and the 'selfTestFail' condition becomes true at the same time, then it must be globally true from that point onward that the system is not 'ventilating'. This accurately captures the requirement that if the self-test fails while in SelfTest Mode, the controller shall never be able to proceed to ventilation.

The natural language statement "If the SelfTest fails, the controller shall not be able to proceed to ventilation" describes a safety property. It implies that if a specific condition, the self-test failing, occurs at any point, then a subsequent operation, ventilation, must never be initiated or occur thereafter. The LTL formula `G (selfTestFail -> G !ventilating)` captures this precisely. `selfTestFail` represents the condition of the self-test failing. The outer `G` (Globally) ensures that this rule applies at all times. The implication `->` states that if `selfTestFail` becomes true, then the consequent must hold. The inner `G !ventilating` means that from the moment `selfTestFail` is true, it must always be the case that `ventilating` is false, meaning ventilation will never proceed.

The LTL property "G (selfTestFail -> G !ventilating)" captures the given requirement. The outer "G" (Globally) ensures that this rule is continuously enforced throughout the system's operation. "selfTestFail" represents the condition "If the SelfTest fails." The "->" (implication) indicates that if the SelfTest fails, a specific consequence must follow. The inner "G !ventilating" means "the controller shall not be able to proceed to ventilation." This ensures that from the moment "selfTestFail" becomes true, the system must not be in the "ventilating" state at that point and at any subsequent point in time, thereby preventing ventilation from starting or continuing after a self-test failure.

The property states that if the self-test fails ('selfTestFail'), then it must always be the case that ventilation is not active ('!ventilating'). The 'G' operator ensures that 'not ventilating' holds globally from the point the self-test fails, meaning the system shall not proceed to ventilation at any future time. This directly captures the conditional requirement that a failed self-test prevents subsequent ventilation.

The statement "If the SelfTest fails, the controller shall not be able to proceed to ventilation" implies a global constraint. It means that at any point in time, if the SelfTest fails, then the system must not be in the 'ventilating' state. This is captured by a global implication: G (selfTestFail -> !ventilating). The 'G' operator ensures that this condition holds true at all times in the future. The '->' operator represents the 'if-then' relationship, and '!ventilating' ensures that ventilation is prevented when 'selfTestFail' is true.

The property states that globally, if the SelfTest fails, then ventilation shall not be active. The 'G' (globally) operator ensures this condition holds at all times. The '->' (implication) operator captures the 'if...then' relationship, linking the 'selfTestFail' condition to the 'not ventilating' outcome.
